title: Remote Packaging Designer Jobs in Canada | Montreal Jobs
description: Find remote packaging and label design positions in Canada. Discover CPG companies, agencies specializing in packaging, typical salaries.

mainEntity.name: What is the average salary for a remote packaging designer in Canada?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Canada, the average salary for a remote packaging designer ranges from $60,000 to $85,000 CAD per year. Senior positions at major CPG (Consumer Packaged Goods) brands or specialized design agencies can exceed $95,000 CAD, while junior or intermediate roles typically start around $50,000 to $65,000 CAD.

mainEntity.name: What software skills are strictly required for packaging design?

acceptedAnswer.text: Adobe Illustrator is non-negotiable, particularly proficiency with die-lines, bleeds, and vector art. Adobe Photoshop is essential for mockups and photo retouching. Increasingly, 3D software like Adobe Dimension, Cinema 4D, or Blender is requested for rendering realistic packaging concepts before physical prototyping.

mainEntity.name: Is it possible to find 100% remote packaging design jobs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but they are less common than general graphic design roles because packaging often requires evaluating physical printed samples, materials, and structural integrity. However, many Canadian agencies and CPG companies now offer fully remote or hybrid roles where physical proofs are mailed directly to the designer's home office.

mainEntity.name: What should I include in a packaging design portfolio?

acceptedAnswer.text: Your portfolio must show the entire process: initial concepts, flat die-lines with technical callouts, high-quality 3D renders, and photographs of the final printed product on shelves if possible. Demonstrating an understanding of print production (spot colors, varnishes, sustainable materials) is crucial.
